title: Bug #2383 got revived: Task argument inherits width of value that was passed to it
body:
I don't know when and how this happened. @zachjs made a testcase for it when he fixed it.

## Steps to reproduce the issue

test.v:
```
module test(input wire [7:0] a, output reg [8:0] b);
	task t(input wire [7:0] c, input wire d);
		b = { c, d };
	endtask
	always @* t(a, 0);
endmodule
```
Run:
`yosys -p "synth_ice40 -blif test.blif" test.v`

## Expected behavior

I would expect the output port b[0] to be 0; and b[8:1] to equal a[7:0].
Snippet from test.blif:
```
...
.names a[0] b[1]
1 1
.names a[1] b[2]
1 1
.names a[2] b[3]
1 1
.names a[3] b[4]
1 1
...

```
## Actual behavior

All nine bits of output port b are zero.
Snippet from test.blif:
```
...
.names $false b[0]
1 1
.names $false b[1]
1 1
.names $false b[2]
1 1
.names $false b[3]
1 1
...

```

In comparison to the original version of this bug (#2383), the workaround of setting the range of the argument d to [0:0] doesn't work anymore.
